rule: All messaging services must support asynchronous delivery
rationale: Enable scalability for millions of concurrent conversations
legacyViolation: Synchronous message processing causes blocking bottlenecks
migratedApproach: Event-driven microservices with Redis/Kafka message queues

rule: All messages must support end-to-end encryption (E2E)
rationale: Ensure user privacy for sensitive conversations
legacyViolation: Messages stored in plaintext in central database
migratedApproach: Signal Protocol implementation with key exchange

rationale: Provide near real-time user experience for conversations

rule: Support Read Receipts and Presence (online status)
rationale: Match modern user expectations in messaging apps
legacyViolation: No read receipts or presence tracking available

rule: Automatic conversation archival after 90 days of inactivity
rationale: Optimize active storage while preserving history
legacyViolation: All conversations stored indefinitely in primary database
migratedApproach: Time-based archival to cold storage with indexed retrieval
